source  
the function SumSquareCubeProduct takes a slice of integers as input and returns an ordered pair of integers. the first element of the pair is the summation of squares of all the elements in the slice, and the second element is the multiplication of cubes of all the elements in the slice. if the slice is empty, the sum of squares is set to null and the product of cubes is set to unity. the function is designed to handle both empty and non-empty slices efficiently. *** the function begins by checking if the slice is empty. if it is, the sum of squares is null and the product of cubes is 1. *** otherwise, the function iterates through each element in the slice, calculates the square of each element and accumulates the sum, and calculates the cube of each element and accumulates the product. *** the summation of squares is computed by initializing a variable to 0 and adding the square of each element to it. *** the multiplication of cubes is computed by initializing a variable to 1 and multiplying it by the cube of each element. *** the function returns the computed sum of squares and product of cubes as an ordered pair. *** the function is designed to handle edge cases such as empty slices and single-element slices.
